steps.title: Recognize the form of the linear equation
steps.content: The equation $d = 16t$ is a linear relationship where 16 is the coefficient of $t$. In a linear function, the coefficient of the independent variable represents how much the dependent variable changes for each unit increase of the independent variable.

steps.title: Interpret the coefficient as a rate
steps.content: The coefficient 16 means: when $t$ increases by 1 second, $d$ increases by 16 inches. Since $d$ is distance in inches and $t$ is time in seconds, the rate of change is 16 inches per second—the speed at which the object is moving. That matches choice C — C is correct.
